title: AI-901 Mock Exam 1: Foundry Implementation Scenarios
description: Challenging unofficial AI-901 practice focused on Microsoft Foundry implementation, agents, tools, model configuration, responsible AI, and information extraction. Based on the public Microsoft AI-901 skills guide, Microsoft Foundry, Agent Service, and Content Understanding documentation. Original practice questions; not official exam content.

items.prompt: A developer is building a customer-support assistant in Microsoft Foundry. The assistant must answer only from uploaded product manuals and must say when the manuals do not contain the answer. Which design should the developer choose?
items.answer: Create a prompt agent with file search grounded on the manuals and instructions to answer only from retrieved content
options: Create a prompt agent with file search grounded on the manuals and instructions to answer only from retrieved content
Prompt agent
Hosted agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process

items.prompt: A team has an existing LangGraph agent running in AKS. They want to keep the orchestration code but use Foundry models and built-in tools through a single endpoint. What should they call from their existing process?
options: The Responses API
Hosted agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent

items.prompt: A company wants to build an internal HR assistant quickly in the Foundry portal. It needs instructions, model selection, file search, and no custom orchestration code. Which agent type is the best fit?
options: Prompt agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint

items.prompt: A developer needs a Foundry agent with custom Python orchestration, package dependencies, and a managed endpoint with scaling and observability. Which agent type should they use?
options: Hosted agent
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint
Prompt agent

items.prompt: A Python app must authenticate to a Foundry project endpoint without storing an API key in source control. Which code pattern is most appropriate?
items.answer: Create an AIProjectClient with the project endpoint and DefaultAzureCredential
options: Create an AIProjectClient with the project endpoint and DefaultAzureCredential
OAuth On-Behalf-Of for user-delegated tool access
Microsoft Entra authentication with RBAC scoped to the Foundry project
A browser-exposed API key stored in localStorage

items.prompt: A project endpoint is shown as https://contoso.ai.azure.com/api/projects/support. What is this endpoint used for in a Foundry SDK client?
items.answer: Connecting the client to a specific Foundry project for model, agent, and tool calls
options: Connecting the client to a specific Foundry project for model, agent, and tool calls
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks

items.prompt: A developer needs to choose a model for extracting fields from short support messages at very high volume. Accuracy requirements are modest and latency/cost are important. Which model selection approach is most appropriate?
items.answer: Use a smaller faster model that meets quality targets after evaluation
options: Use a smaller faster model that meets quality targets after evaluation
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost

items.prompt: A compliance bot must produce deterministic policy summaries in a fixed format. Which model setting should generally be reduced?
options: Temperature
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining

items.prompt: A chat app sometimes returns long answers that overflow the mobile UI. Which model parameter should the developer configure?
options: Maximum output tokens
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A system prompt tells an assistant not to reveal internal policy. A user asks it to ignore all previous instructions and disclose the policy. What should govern the response?
items.answer: The system instructions and safety controls should still take precedence
options: The system instructions and safety controls should still take precedence
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools

items.prompt: An agent can call a refund API. Which control is most important before enabling the tool in production?
items.answer: Validate tool inputs and restrict tool access with identity and authorization controls
options: Validate tool inputs and restrict tool access with identity and authorization controls
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis

items.prompt: An operations team needs to debug why an agent called a database tool before answering. Which Foundry capability is most useful?
items.answer: Tracing and observability of model and tool steps
options: Tracing and observability of model and tool steps
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost

items.prompt: A company wants stable releases of an agent so client apps do not unexpectedly change behavior when developers test new instructions. What should they use?
options: Agent versioning and publishing
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior

items.prompt: An app sends customer records to a model. Which responsible AI practice best addresses privacy and security?
items.answer: Minimize and protect sensitive data before it is sent or logged
options: Minimize and protect sensitive data before it is sent or logged
Reliability and safety
Privacy and security
Inclusiveness

items.prompt: A hiring-screening model performs worse for one demographic group. Which responsible AI principle is most directly involved?
options: Fairness
Privacy and security
Inclusiveness
Transparency

items.prompt: A loan assistant must let users understand when AI is involved and what data influenced a recommendation. Which principle is emphasized?
options: Transparency
Inclusiveness
Accountability
Fairness

items.prompt: An AI system needs assigned owners, review processes, and escalation paths for harmful outcomes. Which principle is emphasized?
options: Accountability
Transparency
Fairness
Reliability and safety

items.prompt: A public-sector service uses an AI form assistant. It must be usable by people with disabilities and people with different language needs. Which principle is emphasized?
options: Inclusiveness
Accountability
Fairness
Reliability and safety

items.prompt: A safety team tests a medical-advice assistant with risky prompts before release and monitors failures after release. Which principle is emphasized?
options: Reliability and safety
Fairness
Privacy and security
Inclusiveness

items.prompt: A support app must identify product names, order IDs, and customer names in emails. Which text analysis capability is most appropriate?
options: Entity detection
Language detection
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis

items.prompt: A dashboard needs to show whether social-media comments are positive, neutral, or negative. Which capability is most appropriate?
options: Sentiment analysis
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction
Summarization

items.prompt: A researcher needs the main themes from thousands of short survey responses. Which capability is most useful?
options: Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis
Summarization
Language detection

items.prompt: A user uploads a 20-page policy and asks for the main obligations in five bullets. Which capability is most directly involved?
options: Summarization
Sentiment analysis
Language detection
Entity detection

items.prompt: A kiosk must accept spoken questions and return spoken answers. Which pipeline is most appropriate?
items.answer: Speech recognition, model response generation, and speech synthesis
options: Speech recognition, model response generation, and speech synthesis
A multimodal image model with no audio handling
Speech translation when source and target languages differ
Speech recognition followed by model processing and speech synthesis

items.prompt: A warehouse app needs to detect whether a package label is visible in a photo and read the label text. Which workload combination is most appropriate?
items.answer: Computer vision with OCR or visual text extraction
options: Computer vision with OCR or visual text extraction
OCR or visual text extraction
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input
A text-only chat model with no visual input

items.prompt: A marketing team wants to create product-background concepts from a text prompt. Which capability should they use?
options: Image generation
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction

items.prompt: An app receives a photo of a circuit board and a text prompt asking what component appears damaged. Which model capability is required?
options: Multimodal visual understanding
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt

items.prompt: A mortgage processor needs vendor name, total amount, due date, and line items from uploaded invoices. Which Foundry Tools capability is most relevant?
options: Content Understanding for document extraction
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled

items.prompt: A media archive needs speaker names, key topics, and timestamps from long videos. Which capability is most relevant?
items.answer: Content Understanding for audio and video
options: Content Understanding for audio and video
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled
Content Understanding with a custom or prebuilt analyzer

items.prompt: A logistics app needs container IDs from yard photos. Which information extraction input type is involved?
options: Image extraction
OCR or visual text extraction
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input
A text-only chat model with no visual input

items.prompt: A developer wants a model to return strict JSON with fields "summary", "sentiment", and "actions". What should the prompt include?
items.answer: A clear output schema and formatting constraints
options: A clear output schema and formatting constraints
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis

items.prompt: A model returns unsupported details when retrieved documents are incomplete. Which change best reduces this risk?
items.answer: Ground the model with retrieval and instruct it to state when information is missing
options: Ground the model with retrieval and instruct it to state when information is missing
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost

items.prompt: An agent needs to calculate totals from uploaded CSV files. Which built-in tool is most appropriate?
options: Code interpreter
Web search
Memory
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication

items.prompt: An agent needs to answer from uploaded PDF policy files. Which built-in tool is most appropriate?
options: File search
Memory
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
A connected MCP server with scoped tools

items.prompt: An agent needs up-to-date public information where policy allows it. Which tool category is most relevant?
options: Web search
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
File search

items.prompt: An agent should remember relevant user preferences across interactions without requiring the user to repeat them every time. Which capability is most relevant?
options: Memory
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
File search
Code interpreter

items.prompt: A developer sees that two models can answer the prompt, but one is much slower. Which factor should guide the deployment choice?
items.answer: Quality requirements balanced against latency and cost
options: Quality requirements balanced against latency and cost
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost

items.prompt: A model deployment works in the portal but the app receives authorization failures. What should the developer check first?
items.answer: The app identity, RBAC or API credential, endpoint, and project access
options: The app identity, RBAC or API credential, endpoint, and project access
Sentiment analysis
Summarization
Language detection

items.prompt: A customer-facing assistant must block unsafe instructions and detect harmful user requests. Which platform capability helps?
options: Content filters and safety controls
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use

items.prompt: A team wants to evaluate if responses are supported by retrieved documents. Which evaluation dimension is most relevant?
options: Groundedness or faithfulness
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools

items.prompt: A developer has to build a minimal TypeScript web client that calls a Foundry agent endpoint. Where should secrets or privileged credentials be kept?
items.answer: On the server side or in managed identity, not in the browser bundle
options: On the server side or in managed identity, not in the browser bundle
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction
Sentiment analysis

items.prompt: A project has strict network isolation requirements. Which Foundry platform area should the team review?
items.answer: Enterprise controls such as RBAC, networking, and policies
options: Enterprise controls such as RBAC, networking, and policies
A browser-exposed API key stored in localStorage
A managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential-based app identity
Anonymous access with tool permissions disabled

items.prompt: A user uploads a receipt photo and wants merchant, date, tax, total, and payment method extracted. Which workload is this?
options: Information extraction from an image
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt
OCR or visual text extraction

items.prompt: A call-center analytics app needs transcripts before it can summarize calls. Which first step is required?
options: Convert speech to text
A multimodal image model with no audio handling
Speech translation when source and target languages differ
Speech recognition followed by model processing and speech synthesis

items.prompt: A training app reads generated feedback aloud after grading a spoken answer. Which capability produces the audio?
options: Speech synthesis
Speech translation when source and target languages differ
Speech recognition followed by model processing and speech synthesis
Speech synthesis only with no transcription step

items.prompt: A prompt includes examples of two customer tickets and exactly formatted desired responses. What prompting technique is being used?
options: Few-shot prompting
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: A prompt says: "You are a support assistant. Answer in exactly three bullets. Do not mention internal policies." What type of instruction is this best suited for?
options: System prompt
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

items.prompt: A developer wants to compare responses from different model deployments before changing production. What should they use first?
items.answer: A Foundry playground or test experience
options: A Foundry playground or test experience
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining

items.prompt: A team needs to swap from one Foundry model to another without rewriting agent orchestration. Which platform capability helps?
items.answer: Common APIs and model catalog access through Foundry
options: Common APIs and model catalog access through Foundry
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: An app returns model output directly into a database update operation. What should the developer add before executing the update?
items.answer: Output validation and guardrails for expected format and values
options: Output validation and guardrails for expected format and values
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation
